decisions.reasoning: Iron Hands threatens a 2HKO with Drain Punch and out-damages Incineroar significantly. Switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to Drain Punch and forces the opponent to switch or risk a super-effective hit.
I outspeed Iron Hands and Moonblast is a guaranteed OHKO, removing the threat immediately.

Since we are in a speed tie and the opponent's Shadow Ball can 2HKO us while we only 2HKO them, staying in is a losing gamble. Switching to Incineroar allows us to safely pivot into a resist and potentially gain momentum with Intimidate.
Flare Blitz is a guaranteed OHKO on Flutter Mane, removing a major offensive threat immediately. Prioritizing the KO is the optimal play according to the decision priorities.
Flare Blitz is a guaranteed OHKO on the Flutter Mane, which is currently at 2% HP. This secures the KO and removes the immediate threat.
Incineroar is at 4% HP and will be KO'd by any chip damage; Flare Blitz maximizes damage output before fainting to ensure Tornadus-Therian is weakened for the rest of the team.
Incineroar has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Flutter Mane is the best choice to pressure Tornadus-Therian with its high speed and special attack.
Moonblast is the strongest move and has a high chance to 2HKO Tornadus-Therian. Since I outspeed and the opponent cannot OHKO me, attacking is the optimal play to maintain momentum.
Moonblast is a guaranteed 2HKO on Tornadus-Therian, and since I outspeed it, I can secure the KO this turn or next before it can threaten me with a 3HKO.
The opponent's Tornadus-Therian is at 11% HP, well within the range for Moonblast to secure a KO, which removes their pivot and prevents them from utilizing Regenerator.
